citation_and_locator: Au Yeung, Wai-ho. 2001. `動詞後置成份「住得開」與複句的關係`. Workshop on Cantonese Verbal Complements, Linguistic Society of Hong Kong; abstract pp. 3-4, examples (16b), (17b), and (20b)
what_it_supports: Calls `一邊…一邊…` a conjunction connecting two actions; gives same-subject profiles, `住` inside the first event, and a distinct-subject `我…你…` profile contrasted with the same-subject restriction of the `住` complex sentence.
limit: A workshop abstract with author judgments does not establish a full constituency theory, complete event/aspect compatibility, frequency, omission, prosody, or regional distribution.

citation_and_locator: Chinese University of Hong Kong Independent Learning Centre. Cantonese Express, Daily Conversation 5 comparison page, accessed 2026-07-23
what_it_supports: Lists `一便／邊／面` with `jat1 bin6/bin1/min6` and exact paired `一邊睇電視，一邊食飯` and `一邊食一邊傾` activity examples.
limit: A pedagogical comparison does not prove free interchangeability of spellings/readings, exhaustive syntax, subject constraints, omission, or productivity.

citation_and_locator: Wong, Anita Mei-Yin. 2023. Understanding Development and Disorder in Cantonese Using Language Sample Analysis. Routledge; GACS temporal conjunctions, printed p. 62 (PDF p. 72)
what_it_supports: Separately lists `一路…一路…` as a Cantonese temporal conjunction and gives `妹妹一路睇電視一路做功課` with a while translation.
limit: It does not compare `一路` with `一邊` or establish that they share all syntax, event restrictions, or register.
